title: README still describes the pre-v3 `issues` bookmark storage model
body:
# Goal

Reconcile `README.md` to the v3 storage model. The README still
describes the pre-cutover `issues` bookmark contract; v3 storage
(shipped 2026-06-24 in epic `bd98097 storage-v3-out-of-tree`)
moved to a per-issue ref namespace under `refs/jjf/*`. The
quickstart at `docs/quickstart.md` is already correct; the
README contradicts it.

# Repro / observed

From the fj issue (claude@github.com):

```
$ jj bookmark list
(empty — no `issues` bookmark)

$ git for-each-ref refs/jjf
... refs/jjf/issues/<id>     # one ref per issue
... refs/jjf/memories/<key>  # one ref per memory
... refs/jjf/meta/format-version  # sentinel
```

Storage is exactly what `docs/quickstart.md` describes — and what
the README does NOT.

# Stale claims in README to fix

1. "Issues live as commits on a dedicated `issues` bookmark in
   the underlying jj repo." — wrong since v3.
2. "**Bookmarks are the unit of sync.** Push/pull a bookmark and
   you've round-tripped a whole issue tracker." — A user
   following this would `jj git push` the bookmark (no-op, since
   there is none) and not round-trip issues. The real refspec is
   `+refs/jjf/*:refs/remotes/<remote>/jjf/*` per quickstart and
   per `Storage::push_v3` at
   `crates/jjf-storage/src/sync_v3.rs:179` and the `jjf push`/`jjf
   pull` CLI verbs.
3. Architecture / "Why jj-native" sections lean on the bookmark
   model and need re-anchoring on the ref-namespace model.

# Fix shape

- Edit `README.md` so the storage paragraphs match the v3 model:
  per-issue refs under `refs/jjf/issues/<id>`, per-memory refs
  under `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`, the format-version sentinel
  under `refs/jjf/meta/`.
- Replace the "bookmarks are the unit of sync" claim with the
  actual mechanism: `jjf push <remote>` / `jjf pull <remote>`
  round-trip the whole `refs/jjf/*` namespace via standard git
  transport.
- A one-line footnote about the v3 cutover is fine (the README is
  a public-facing pitch, not a changelog).
- Keep the README's tone — the goal is correctness, not a rewrite.

# Acceptance

- [ ] README.md no longer claims an `issues` bookmark exists.
- [ ] README.md's sync section matches the `refs/jjf/*` ref-
      namespace model and points at `jjf push` / `jjf pull` as
      the operator surface.
- [ ] README matches `docs/quickstart.md` on the storage model.

# Cross-refs

- Source: github.com/myers/jjforge issue #1.
- `docs/quickstart.md` is the source of truth.
- v3 storage epic `bd98097 storage-v3-out-of-tree` (closed
  2026-06-24).
- v3 push impl: `crates/jjf-storage/src/sync_v3.rs:179`.
